hero.label: Beginner's Guide
hero.subtitle: Horse racing might seem complicated, but it's actually quite simple: several horses start at the same time, and the one that crosses the finish line first wins.

whatAreRaces.title: What Are Horse Races
whatAreRaces.p1: Most races feature 8–12 horses. They all start simultaneously from special gates and run along the same track.
whatAreRaces.quote: The horse that crosses the finish line first wins. A single race lasts 1 to 3 minutes — the result is known almost instantly.
whatAreRaces.gatesDesc: All horses start at the same time
whatAreRaces.turnDesc: This is where position is decided

trackDiagram.label: How the Track Works
trackDiagram.title: Anatomy of a Racecourse
trackDiagram.description: Most racecourses have an oval shape. The decisive burst happens on the home straight — the final 200–400 meters.

distances.label: Race Types
distances.title: Three Main Distances
distances.sprintDesc: These are the fastest races. Horses reach top speed right away, and the race is over in about a minute. Spectators love them because they're incredibly dynamic.
distances.middleDesc: The most popular type of race. Here, it's not just about speed but also about pacing correctly. Many famous tournaments are run at these distances.
distances.longDesc: This is a true test of endurance. The jockey must manage the horse's energy wisely so it can accelerate at the finish.

funFacts.label: Fun Facts
funFacts.title: Racing in Numbers
funFacts.speedDesc: Average speed of a racehorse in a sprint. That's faster than the speed limit in the city.
funFacts.weightDesc: Average weight of a thoroughbred racehorse. Meanwhile, a jockey weighs only 50–55 kg.
funFacts.historyDesc: Horse racing as an organized sport has existed since the 18th century. The first Derby took place in 1780.
funFacts.prizeDesc: The Saudi Cup is the richest race in the world with a prize fund of $35 million.

calculator.label: Try It Yourself
calculator.title: Betting
calculator.description: Move the sliders to see how the potential winnings change depending on the bet amount and odds.

starsSection.label: More About the Favorites
starsSection.title: Racing Stars of 2026
starsSection.subtitle: Learn more about each horse — their style, history, and why they're worth watching.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.title: Who's Your Favorite?
comparison.subtitle: Compare key metrics of the five stars of the season to make an informed choice.

steps.label: How to Start
steps.title: Three Simple Steps
steps.step1Desc: Open the schedule and pick a race that interests you. Pay attention to the start time and the list of runners.
steps.step2Desc: Check the list of horses, their odds, and recent performance stats. This will help you make an informed choice.
steps.step3Desc: Pick a horse and enter the amount. Once confirmed, the bet is locked in, and all that's left is to wait for the race to start.

photoDivider.hippodrome: A modern racecourse — an arena for hundreds of thousands of spectators
photoDivider.emotion: Every race is a storm of emotion and strategy
photoDivider.journey: Ready to start your journey into the world of horse racing?

footer.description: Everything about horse racing: from the basics to the main events of the season
footer.copyright: © 2026 RaceHub. Informational resource. All data is for reference purposes only.

heroDark.label: Season 2026
heroDark.subtitle: It's not just a race. It's the moment when everything comes down to seconds, instinct, and character — horse and human.

seasonIntro.p1: Horse racing has existed for centuries, yet it still evokes the same emotions: tension, excitement, and that unforgettable 'wow' moment at the finish.
seasonIntro.p2: The best racehorses train for years to deliver the perfect combination of speed, endurance, and tactics. Millions of fans follow their performances.
seasonIntro.readyLine2: Feel the thrill of the start.
seasonIntro.readyLine3: And catch that rush of excitement.

grandNationalPreview.label: Upcoming Event · April 11, 2026
grandNationalPreview.title: Grand National Festival
grandNationalPreview.subtitle: Aintree, Liverpool · 16:00 BST · Willie Mullins enters 9 horses (26% of the field). I Am Maximus attempts to become the first horse with three consecutive wins.
grandNationalPreview.insight: 23 of 34 runners train in Ireland. The favorite wins the Grand National only 17% of the time — it's one of the most unpredictable races in the world.

grandNational.description: The most famous steeplechase in the world. 4 miles, 30 fences.

kentuckyDerby.description: "The most exciting two minutes in sports." A race for three-year-olds over 2 km.

preakness.description: The second leg of the Triple Crown. Strategy decides everything.

arc.description: Europe's premier race. The world's best compete at Longchamp.

breedersCup.description: The grand finale of the season — the world championship of horse racing.

historicalStats.label: Analytics
historicalStats.title: Grand National Patterns
historicalStats.subtitle: 71 years of statistics — what actually affects the outcome
historicalStats.favoritesText: The favorite won 12 out of 71 post-war races. In 37 cases, the favorite didn't even finish.
historicalStats.lightHorses: Lighter horses statistically win more often. From 1989 to 2004, no horse carrying 11+ stone won.

derbyContenders.label: Kentucky Derby 2026 · May 2
derbyContenders.title: Top Contenders
derbyContenders.subtitle: The leading four based on G1 prep race results

raceEvents.title: Major Races of 2026
raceEvents.subtitle: This is where favorites take the stage, new stars are born, and all predictions are shattered.

photoDividerDark.five: Five major events of the season across three continents
photoDividerDark.history: Every race is a story written at full speed

iroko.description: Iroko is a classic 'silent killer' on the course: he doesn't stand out, but consistently finishes among the leaders.
iroko.why: This is the pick for those who want to minimize risk and play it safe. Light weight and an experienced jockey make him especially dangerous over long distances.

commandment.description: Commandment is all about explosive speed and pressure from the very start. His Risen Star Stakes victory made him one of the most talked-about young contenders.
commandment.why: Perfect for those who crave adrenaline: a bet on him is always 'all or nothing.' Paired with a strong jockey, he can set a pace that simply breaks the race apart.

barnes.description: Barnes is a symbol of reliability: after the Kentucky Derby, he proved he can maintain form and hold his own against top competition.
barnes.why: This is the quintessential 'smart pick' — not the most hyped, but often the most rewarding. In the hands of one of America's top jockeys, he becomes even more dangerous.

minnieHauk.description: Minnie Hauk is the embodiment of class and pedigree. She's already being called a future Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe-level star.
minnieHauk.why: Betting on her means backing obvious talent. A world-class experienced jockey only strengthens the feeling that everything is 'under control.'

senorBuscador.description: Senor Buscador is a veteran with character who has fought back into contention time and again when everyone had written him off. G1-level experience makes him dangerous against any field.
senorBuscador.why: This is a comeback story you can't help but root for. With his jockey, he's capable of delivering the maximum even in the toughest races.

grandNational.description: The Grand National is a survival test where favorites often lose to those who simply endured the distance. Every second is filled with tension, and the finish is almost always a story against the odds.

kentuckyDerby.description: The Kentucky Derby is the moment when stars are born. Young horses take to the track with a chance to make history.

preakness.description: After the Derby, this is where the true staying power of a champion is tested. The Preakness is a race where strategy takes center stage, and mistakes are costly.

arc.description: The Arc is Europe's premier race, where the very best meet head to head.

breedersCup.description: The Breeders' Cup is the grand finale of the season — effectively a 'world championship' that gathers the best from every continent.

metadata.beginnersDescription: A complete guide to horse racing: how races work, distances, a betting calculator, and the stars of the 2026 season.
metadata.racesTitle: Races 2026 — Major Events of the Season
metadata.racesDescription: Grand National, Kentucky Derby, Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe, Breeders' Cup. Analytics, odds, real data.
